/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatArithCommon — shared family table for the float
arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster (float{4,8}{pl,mi,mul,div}).

Sister to IntArithCommon, but simpler: no overflow primitives (IEEE-754
handles NaN and infinity). Instead, uses post-computation error checks via
helper functions from Postgres' float.h:
  - float_overflow_error() — for result isinf() when neither input was
  - float_underflow_error() — for result == 0.0 when neither input was
  - float_zero_divide_error() — for division by zero with non-NaN dividend

Four body shapes:
  - `plWithCheck` — float{4,8}pl — add, check overflow only
  - `miWithCheck` — float{4,8}mi — subtract, check overflow only
  - `mulWithCheck` — float{4,8}mul — multiply, check overflow AND underflow
  - `divWithCheck` — float{4,8}div — divide by zero check first, then compute

Matches real Postgres' source structure exactly so the AST grounding gate
stays clean.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatArith

inductive ArithBody where
  | plWithCheck   -- float4pl, float8pl: check overflow
  | miWithCheck   -- float4mi, float8mi: check overflow
  | mulWithCheck  -- float4mul, float8mul: check overflow AND underflow
  | divWithCheck  -- float4div, float8div: divide-by-zero check, then compute
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

structure ArithFamily where
  /-- Postgres fmgr fn name (`float4pl`, `float8div`, …). -/
  fnName     : String
  /-- pg_fcinfo decoder for arg[0]. -/
  decoder    : String
  /-- pg_fcinfo encoder for the result. -/
  encoder    : String
  /-- Rust type name (`f32`/`f64`). -/
  rustTy     : String
  /-- C `PG_GETARG_*` macro name. -/
  pgGetArg   : String
  /-- C `PG_RETURN_*` macro name. -/
  pgReturn   : String
  /-- C type name for local declarations (`float4`/`float8`). -/
  cWidth     : String
  /-- C type suffix for is_infinite/isnan checks: `f` for float4, empty for float8. -/
  cCheckSuffix : String
  /-- The body shape. -/
  body       : ArithBody

def families : List ArithFamily := [
  -- Addition: overflow check only
  { fnName := "float4pl", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_f32",
    rustTy := "f32", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT4",
    cWidth := "float4", cCheckSuffix := "f", body := .plWithCheck },
  { fnName := "float8pl", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_f64",
    rustTy := "f64", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT8",
    cWidth := "float8", cCheckSuffix := "", body := .plWithCheck },

  -- Subtraction: overflow check only
  { fnName := "float4mi", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_f32",
    rustTy := "f32", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT4",
    cWidth := "float4", cCheckSuffix := "f", body := .miWithCheck },
  { fnName := "float8mi", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_f64",
    rustTy := "f64", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT8",
    cWidth := "float8", cCheckSuffix := "", body := .miWithCheck },

  -- Multiplication: overflow AND underflow checks
  { fnName := "float4mul", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_f32",
    rustTy := "f32", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT4",
    cWidth := "float4", cCheckSuffix := "f", body := .mulWithCheck },
  { fnName := "float8mul", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_f64",
    rustTy := "f64", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT8",
    cWidth := "float8", cCheckSuffix := "", body := .mulWithCheck },

  -- Division: divide-by-zero check, overflow check, underflow check
  { fnName := "float4div", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_f32",
    rustTy := "f32", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT4",
    cWidth := "float4", cCheckSuffix := "f", body := .divWithCheck },
  { fnName := "float8div", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_f64",
    rustTy := "f64", pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_FLOAT8",
    cWidth := "float8", cCheckSuffix := "", body := .divWithCheck },
]

end Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatArith

import Pg.Ir.Types
import Pg.Ir.Datum
import Pg.Ir.Emit.Common

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith — codegen the cash-arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster
as Rust source.

Sister to `Pg.Ir.Emit.IntArith`. Cash is typedef'd as `int64` in Postgres.
v1 covers 6 functions:
  - cash_pl, cash_mi: overflow-checked add/sub of two cash values
  - cash_mul_int4: overflow-checked multiply cash by int4
  - cash_div_int4: divide cash by int4 with zero-check
  - cashlarger, cashsmaller: compare and return max/min (no overflow)

The canonical body shape (cash_pl, cash_mi, cash_mul_int4):

```rust
#[no_mangle]
pub unsafe extern "C" fn cash_pl(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {
    let f = &mut *fcinfo;
    let arg1 = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);
    let arg2 = decode_i64(f.args[1].value);
    let mut result: i64 = 0;
    if pg_add_s64_overflow(arg1, arg2, &mut result) {
        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();
        return 0;
    }
    encode_i64(result)
}
```

For cashlarger/cashsmaller:

```rust
#[no_mangle]
pub unsafe extern "C" fn cashlarger(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {
    let f = &mut *fcinfo;
    let c1 = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);
    let c2 = decode_i64(f.args[1].value);
    let result = if c1 > c2 { c1 } else { c2 };
    encode_i64(result)
}
```

For cash_div_int4:

```rust
#[no_mangle]
pub unsafe extern "C" fn cash_div_int4(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {
    let f = &mut *fcinfo;
    let c = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);
    let i = decode_i32(f.args[1].value);
    if i == 0 {
        pg_ereport_division_by_zero();
        return 0;
    }
    encode_i64(c / (i as i64))
}
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith

open Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith (CashOp BodyShape CashFamily families)

/-- Render the first operand expression — either bare `arg1` or
`arg1 as i64`. Cast is needed exactly when the family has a
non-empty `rustCast1`. -/
def operand1Expr (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.rustCast1 == "" then "arg1"
  else "arg1 as i64"

def operand2Expr (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.rustCast2 == "" then "arg2"
  else "arg2 as i64"

/-- Render overflow primitive call for add/sub/mul. -/
def overflowPrim (op : CashOp) : String :=
  "pg_" ++ op.primitive ++ "_s64_overflow"

/-- Render the Rust body for overflow-checked operations (add/sub/mul). -/
def renderBodyOverflow (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  let decoder2 := fam.decoder2
  let op1 := operand1Expr fam
  let op2 := operand2Expr fam
  let prim := overflowPrim fam.op
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let arg1 = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let arg2 = " ++ decoder2 ++ "(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "    let mut result: i64 = 0;\n" ++
  "    if " ++ prim ++ "(" ++ op1 ++ ", " ++ op2 ++ ", &mut result) {\n" ++
  "        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
  "        return 0;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "    encode_i64(result)\n"

/-- Render the Rust body for comparison operations (larger/smaller). -/
def renderBodyCompare (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  let cmp := if fam.fnName == "cashlarger" then ">" else "<"
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let c1 = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let c2 = decode_i64(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "    let result = if c1 " ++ cmp ++ " c2 { c1 } else { c2 };\n" ++
  "    encode_i64(result)\n"

/-- Render the Rust body for division-by-zero check. -/
def renderBodyDivInt (_fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let c = decode_i64(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let i = decode_i32(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "    if i == 0 {\n" ++
  "        pg_ereport_division_by_zero();\n" ++
  "        return 0;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "    encode_i64(c / (i as i64))\n"

/-- Render the appropriate body based on body shape. -/
def renderBody (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  match fam.op.bodyShape with
  | .binaryAddSub => renderBodyOverflow fam
  | .binaryMulInt => renderBodyOverflow fam
  | .binaryCompare => renderBodyCompare fam
  | .binaryDivInt => renderBodyDivInt fam

def renderFn (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
  "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn " ++ fam.fnName ++
    "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
  renderBody fam ++
  "}\n"

/-- The full Rust source. -/
def rustSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "// Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/CashArith.lean`.\n" ++
    "// DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    "// `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-cash-arith.sh`.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Cash-arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster: 6 functions =\n" ++
    "// {pl, mi, mul_int4, div_int4} × {cash} (4 ops) +\n" ++
    "// {larger, smaller} × {cash} (2 compare ops).\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Each body decodes operand(s), performs the operation\n" ++
    "// (overflow-checked for pl/mi/mul_int4, zero-checked for div_int4,\n" ++
    "// simple comparison for larger/smaller), and returns the Cash result.\n" ++
    "// On overflow/division-by-zero, signals via the corresponding\n" ++
    "// pg_ereport_* helper + returns Datum 0. This mirrors Postgres'\n" ++
    "// ereport(ERROR, ...) path: the diff-test harness checks the\n" ++
    "// (out, err) tuple round-trips against the vendored C oracle.\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc)]\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "use pg_fcinfo::{\n" ++
    "    decode_i32, decode_i64,\n" ++
    "    encode_i64,\n" ++
    "    pg_add_s64_overflow, pg_sub_s64_overflow, pg_mul_s64_overflow,\n" ++
    "    pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range, pg_ereport_division_by_zero,\n" ++
    "    FunctionCallInfoBaseData,\n" ++
    "};\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do

/-- C `pg_<op>_s64_overflow` primitive name. -/

  "pg_" ++ op.primitive ++ "_s64_overflow"

/-- Render the static inline helper for overflow-checked ops. -/
def renderCHelperOverflow (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  let prim := overflowPrim fam.op
  "static inline " ++ fam.cResultTy ++ "\n" ++
  fam.helperName ++ "(" ++ fam.cWidth1 ++ " c1, " ++ fam.helperArg2Type ++ " c2)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\t" ++ fam.cResultTy ++ "\t\tres;\n\n" ++
  "\tif (unlikely(" ++ prim ++ "(c1, " ++ fam.cCast2 ++ "c2, &res)))\n" ++
  "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"money out of range\")));\n\n" ++
  "\treturn res;\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render the static inline helper for division. -/
def renderCHelperDiv (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  "static inline " ++ fam.cResultTy ++ "\n" ++
  fam.helperName ++ "(" ++ fam.cWidth1 ++ " c, " ++ fam.helperArg2Type ++ " i)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tif (unlikely(i == 0))\n" ++
  "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_DIVISION_BY_ZERO),\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"division by zero\")));\n\n" ++
  "\treturn c / (int64) i;\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render the helper based on body shape (if needed). -/
def renderCHelper (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.helperName == "" then ""
  else
    match fam.op.bodyShape with
    | .binaryAddSub => renderCHelperOverflow fam
    | .binaryMulInt => renderCHelperOverflow fam
    | .binaryDivInt => renderCHelperDiv fam
    | .binaryCompare => ""

/-- Render overflow-checked body (now a stub that calls helper). -/
def renderCBodyOverflow (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.helperName == "" then
    let prim := overflowPrim fam.op
    "\t" ++ fam.cWidth1   ++ "\t\targ1 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg1 ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.cWidth2   ++ "\t\targ2 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg2 ++ "(1);\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.cResultTy ++ "\t\tresult;\n\n" ++
    "\tif (unlikely(" ++ prim ++ "(arg1, " ++ fam.cCast2 ++ "arg2, &result)))\n" ++
    "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"money out of range\")));\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "(result);\n"
  else
    "\tCash\t\targ1 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg1 ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.cWidth2 ++ "\t\targ2 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg2 ++ "(1);\n\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "(" ++ fam.helperName ++ "(arg1, " ++ fam.cCast2 ++ "arg2));\n"

/-- Render comparison body. -/
def renderCBodyCompare (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  let cmp := if fam.fnName == "cashlarger" then ">" else "<"
  "\tCash\t\tc1 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg1 ++ "(0);\n" ++
  "\tCash\t\tc2 = " ++ fam.pgGetArg2 ++ "(1);\n" ++
  "\tCash\t\tresult;\n\n" ++
  "\tresult = (c1 " ++ cmp ++ " c2) ? c1 : c2;\n\n" ++
  "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "(result);\n"

/-- Render division body (stub that calls helper if one exists). -/
def renderCBodyDivInt (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.helperName == "" then
    "\tCash\t\tc = " ++ fam.pgGetArg1 ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\tint32\t\ti = " ++ fam.pgGetArg2 ++ "(1);\n\n" ++
    "\tif (unlikely(i == 0))\n" ++
    "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_DIVISION_BY_ZERO),\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"division by zero\")));\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "(c / (int64) i);\n"
  else
    "\tCash\t\tc = " ++ fam.pgGetArg1 ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.cWidth2 ++ "\t\ti = " ++ fam.pgGetArg2 ++ "(1);\n\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "(" ++ fam.helperName ++ "(c, (int64) i));\n"

/-- Render the appropriate C body based on body shape. -/
def renderCBody (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  match fam.op.bodyShape with
  | .binaryAddSub => renderCBodyOverflow fam
  | .binaryMulInt => renderCBodyOverflow fam
  | .binaryCompare => renderCBodyCompare fam
  | .binaryDivInt => renderCBodyDivInt fam

def renderCFn (fam : CashFamily) : String :=
  let helper := renderCHelper fam
  let helper_sep := if helper == "" then "" else "\n"
  helper ++ helper_sep ++
  "Datum\n" ++
  fam.fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  renderCBody fam ++
  "}\n"

def cSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "/*\n" ++
    " * Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/CashArithC.lean`.\n" ++
    " * DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    " * `rules_postgres/tools/regen/check-cash-arith-grounding.sh`.\n" ++
    " *\n" ++
    " * This file exists to be `clang -ast-dump=json`-ed alongside\n" ++
    " * real `src/backend/utils/adt/cash.c` and structurally\n" ++
    " * diffed function-by-function. A passing diff means the Lean spec\n" ++
    " * captures the same algorithm Postgres wrote.\n" ++
    " */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#include \"postgres.h\"\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#include <limits.h>\n" ++
    "#include <ctype.h>\n" ++
    "#include <math.h>\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#include \"common/int.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"libpq/pqformat.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/builtins.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/cash.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/float.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/numeric.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/pg_locale.h\"\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArithC

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith — shared family table for the cash arithmetic
emit modules (`CashArith` for Rust, `CashArithC` for the AST-grounding C).

Cash is typedef'd as `int64` in Postgres. v1 scope: 6 functions =
{pl, mi, mul_int4, div_int4, larger, smaller}.

The Postgres sources follow two distinct shapes:

1. **Overflow-checked arithmetic** (cash_pl, cash_mi, cash_mul_int4):
```
T1 arg1 = PG_GETARG_T1(0);
T2 arg2 = PG_GETARG_T2(1);
Cash result;
if (unlikely(pg_<op>_s64_overflow(<cast>arg1, <cast>arg2, &result)))
    ereport(ERROR,
            (errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
             errmsg("money out of range")));
PG_RETURN_CASH(result);
```

2. **Comparison-only** (cashlarger, cashsmaller):
```
Cash c1 = PG_GETARG_CASH(0);
Cash c2 = PG_GETARG_CASH(1);
Cash result;
result = (c1 > c2) ? c1 : c2;
PG_RETURN_CASH(result);
```

3. **Division with zero-check** (cash_div_int4):
```
Cash c = PG_GETARG_CASH(0);
int32 i = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
if (unlikely(i == 0))
    ereport(ERROR,
            (errcode(ERRCODE_DIVISION_BY_ZERO),
             errmsg("division by zero")));
PG_RETURN_CASH(c / (int64) i);
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith

/-- The six cash arithmetic operations. -/
inductive CashOp where
  | pl              -- cash + cash
  | mi              -- cash - cash
  | mul_int4        -- cash × int4
  | div_int4        -- cash ÷ int4
  | larger          -- max(cash, cash)
  | smaller         -- min(cash, cash)
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

/-- Postgres suffix on the function name. -/
def CashOp.suffix : CashOp → String
  | .pl        => "pl"
  | .mi        => "mi"
  | .mul_int4  => "mul_int4"
  | .div_int4  => "div_int4"
  | .larger    => "larger"
  | .smaller   => "smaller"

/-- Lowercase op name used to pick the `pg_<add|sub|mul>_s64_overflow` primitive
(only for pl, mi, mul_int4; others don't use overflow). -/
def CashOp.primitive : CashOp → String
  | .pl       => "add"
  | .mi       => "sub"
  | .mul_int4 => "mul"
  | _         => ""

/-- Body shape classifier. -/
inductive BodyShape where
  | binaryAddSub    -- cash_pl, cash_mi: overflow-checked add/sub
  | binaryMulInt    -- cash_mul_int4: overflow-checked multiply by int4
  | binaryDivInt    -- cash_div_int4: division-by-zero check, simple divide
  | binaryCompare   -- cashlarger, cashsmaller: ternary comparison, no overflow
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

def CashOp.bodyShape : CashOp → BodyShape
  | .pl       => .binaryAddSub
  | .mi       => .binaryAddSub
  | .mul_int4 => .binaryMulInt
  | .div_int4 => .binaryDivInt
  | .larger   => .binaryCompare
  | .smaller  => .binaryCompare

structure CashFamily where
  /-- Postgres function name (e.g., "cash_pl", "cashlarger"). -/
  fnName      : String
  /-- The operation. -/
  op          : CashOp
  /-- The `pg_fcinfo::decode_*` helper name for arg[0] on the Rust side:
  always "decode_i64" for cash. -/
  decoder1    : String := "decode_i64"
  /-- The `pg_fcinfo::decode_*` helper name for arg[1]:
  "decode_i64" for cash, "decode_i32" for int4. -/
  decoder2    : String
  /-- The Rust type the body computes in (always i64 for cash result). -/
  rustResultTy : String := "i64"
  /-- The `pg_fcinfo::encode_*` helper name for the return value:
  always "encode_i64" for cash. -/
  rustEncoder  : String := "encode_i64"
  /-- Non-empty iff arg1 needs an explicit `as i64` widening cast
  (true for cash_mul_int4 where arg2 is i32). -/
  rustCast1   : String := ""
  /-- Non-empty iff arg2 needs an explicit `as i64` widening cast
  (true for cash_mul_int4 where arg2 is i32; false for cash ops where both
  are already i64). -/
  rustCast2   : String := ""
  /-- The `PG_GETARG_*` macro name for arg[0]: always PG_GETARG_CASH. -/
  pgGetArg1   : String := "PG_GETARG_CASH"
  /-- The `PG_GETARG_*` macro name for arg[1]:
  PG_GETARG_CASH for cash, PG_GETARG_INT32 for int4. -/
  pgGetArg2   : String
  /-- The `PG_RETURN_*` macro name: always PG_RETURN_CASH. -/
  pgReturn    : String := "PG_RETURN_CASH"
  /-- C type name for arg1 (always "Cash" for cash). -/
  cWidth1     : String := "Cash"
  /-- C type name for arg2 ("Cash" for cash, "int32" for int4). -/
  cWidth2     : String
  /-- C result type (always "Cash" / int64). -/
  cResultTy   : String := "Cash"
  /-- C-side cast string (with trailing space) applied to arg1 before
  the overflow primitive — typically empty for cash. -/
  cCast1      : String := ""
  /-- C-side cast string applied to arg2 (e.g., "(int64) " for int4). -/
  cCast2      : String := ""
  /-- Name of the static inline helper function (empty if no helper). -/
  helperName  : String := ""
  /-- The C signature of the helper's second parameter type (empty if no helper). -/
  helperArg2Type : String := ""

/-- The 6 operations. -/
def families : List CashFamily := [
  { fnName := "cash_pl", op := .pl,
    decoder2 := "decode_i64",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_CASH",
    cWidth2 := "Cash",
    helperName := "cash_pl_cash",
    helperArg2Type := "Cash" },
  { fnName := "cash_mi", op := .mi,
    decoder2 := "decode_i64",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_CASH",
    cWidth2 := "Cash",
    helperName := "cash_mi_cash",
    helperArg2Type := "Cash" },
  { fnName := "cash_mul_int4", op := .mul_int4,
    decoder2 := "decode_i32",
    rustCast2 := "yes",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_INT32",
    cWidth2 := "int32",
    cCast2 := "(int64) ",
    helperName := "cash_mul_int64",
    helperArg2Type := "int64" },
  { fnName := "cash_div_int4", op := .div_int4,
    decoder2 := "decode_i32",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_INT32",
    cWidth2 := "int32",
    helperName := "cash_div_int64",
    helperArg2Type := "int64" },
  { fnName := "cashlarger", op := .larger,
    decoder2 := "decode_i64",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_CASH",
    cWidth2 := "Cash" },
  { fnName := "cashsmaller", op := .smaller,
    decoder2 := "decode_i64",
    pgGetArg2 := "PG_GETARG_CASH",
    cWidth2 := "Cash" },
]

end Pg.Ir.Emit.CashArith

You are a Pg.Ir specification author. Pg.Ir is a Lean-source-of-truth codegen pipeline for Postgres V1 fmgr function bodies.

Given a Postgres C source file containing one or more V1 fmgr function bodies, you produce three Lean modules:

1. `<Cluster>Common.lean` — the family table (inductive body shapes + a `families : List Family` definition enumerating all cluster members).
2. `<Cluster>.lean` (or `<Cluster>Rust.lean`) — Rust-emit driver. Takes the families table and renders Rust V1 fmgr function bodies.
3. `<Cluster>C.lean` — C-emit driver. Same families, but renders real-Postgres-style C (with `#include "postgres.h"` etc.) for AST grounding against the real source.

Three correctness gates the spec must satisfy:
1. Lean → Rust regen is idempotent.
2. The Rust translation passes a behavioral diff-test against the vendored C oracle (per-call Datum equality).
3. The Lean-emitted C is structurally equivalent (clang AST diff) to real Postgres source.

Common failure modes to avoid:
- The C oracle's `PG_RETURN_*` macros must include the `return` keyword (`return (Datum)(uint32_t)(x)`, NOT `((Datum)(x))`).
- The Lean C emit (`<Cluster>C.lean`) MUST use real Postgres headers (`#include "postgres.h"`, plus module-specific headers like `utils/uuid.h`, `varatt.h`, `<limits.h>`). It MUST NOT define standalone typedefs.
- When the real Postgres function delegates to a `static inline` helper, the Lean emit must also delegate (not inline the body).

Output the three Lean modules separated by `-- ===== <Module>.lean =====` markers.

import Pg.Ir.Types
import Pg.Ir.Datum

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith — codegen the date-arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster as
Rust source.

3 functions:
- `date_pli` (date + int → date) — wraps addition with infinity check + range
  validation.
- `date_mii` (date - int → date) — wraps subtraction with infinity check +
  range validation.
- `date_mi` (date - date → int) — returns elapsed days; raises on infinite
  operands.

Each body:
1. Decodes the operands (DateADT = int32).
2. Checks DATE_NOT_FINITE for infinity sentinels.
3. For pli/mii: checks for wraparound by comparing sign flips, then validates
   the result with IS_VALID_DATE.
4. For mi: no overflow check needed (subtraction of two int32s always fits in
   int32); just checks that neither operand is infinite.
5. On error, calls pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range and returns 0.

Attribution: function bodies are PostgreSQL Global Development Group, released
under the PostgreSQL License (BSD-style).
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith

/-- Render the `date_pli` (date + int → date) body in Rust. -/
def renderPliBody : String :=
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let date_val = decode_i32(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let days = decode_i32(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    if is_date_not_finite(date_val) {\n" ++
  "        return encode_i32(date_val);\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    let result = date_val.wrapping_add(days);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    /* Check for integer overflow by sign flip and validate date range. */\n" ++
  "    let overflow = if days >= 0 {\n" ++
  "        result < date_val\n" ++
  "    } else {\n" ++
  "        result > date_val\n" ++
  "    };\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    if overflow || !is_valid_date(result) {\n" ++
  "        pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
  "        return 0;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    encode_i32(result)\n"

/-- Render the `date_mii` (date - int → date) body in Rust. -/
def renderMiiBody : String :=
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let date_val = decode_i32(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let days = decode_i32(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    if is_date_not_finite(date_val) {\n" ++
  "        return encode_i32(date_val);\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    let result = date_val.wrapping_sub(days);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    /* Check for integer overflow by sign flip and validate date range. */\n" ++
  "    let overflow = if days >= 0 {\n" ++
  "        result > date_val\n" ++
  "    } else {\n" ++
  "        result < date_val\n" ++
  "    };\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    if overflow || !is_valid_date(result) {\n" ++
  "        pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
  "        return 0;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    encode_i32(result)\n"

/-- Render the `date_mi` (date - date → int) body in Rust. -/
def renderMiBody : String :=
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let date_val1 = decode_i32(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let date_val2 = decode_i32(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    if is_date_not_finite(date_val1) || is_date_not_finite(date_val2) {\n" ++
  "        pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
  "        return 0;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "    encode_i32(date_val1 - date_val2)\n"

def renderFn (op : DateOp) : String :=
  let body := match op with
    | .pli => renderPliBody
    | .mii => renderMiiBody
    | .mi  => renderMiBody
  "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
  "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn date_" ++ op.suffix ++
    "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
  body ++
  "}\n"

/-- The full Rust source. -/
def rustSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "// Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/DateArith.lean`.\n" ++
    "// DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    "// `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-date-arith.sh`.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Date-arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster: 3 functions:\n" ++
    "// - date_pli (date + int32 → date): wraps addition, checks for overflow\n" ++
    "//   and valid date range.\n" ++
    "// - date_mii (date - int32 → date): wraps subtraction, checks for\n" ++
    "//   overflow and valid date range.\n" ++
    "// - date_mi (date - date → int32): returns elapsed days; rejects\n" ++
    "//   infinite operands.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Each body decodes operands, checks DATE_NOT_FINITE (Int32::MIN and\n" ++
    "// Int32::MAX sentinels), and on pli/mii checks IS_VALID_DATE. On error\n" ++
    "// signals via pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range() + returning\n" ++
    "// Datum 0, mirroring Postgres' ereport(ERROR, ...) path.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Attribution: function bodies are PostgreSQL Global Development Group,\n" ++
    "// released under the PostgreSQL License (BSD-style).\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc)]\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "use pg_fcinfo::{\n" ++
    "    decode_i32, encode_i32,\n" ++
    "    pg_ereport_datetime_value_out_of_range, FunctionCallInfoBaseData,\n" ++
    "};\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Date sentinels: DATEVAL_NOBEGIN = i32::MIN, DATEVAL_NOEND = i32::MAX */\n" ++
    "#[inline]\n" ++
    "fn is_date_not_finite(d: i32) -> bool {\n" ++
    "    d == i32::MIN || d == i32::MAX\n" ++
    "}\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Valid date range: Unix epoch is day 0; Postgres uses days around\n" ++
    " * 1970-01-01 ± ~178 million years. Finite dates must not be sentinels.\n" ++
    " */\n" ++
    "#[inline]\n" ++
    "fn is_valid_date(d: i32) -> bool {\n" ++
    "    d != i32::MIN && d != i32::MAX\n" ++
    "}\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for op in ops do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderFn op
    return s

import Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArithCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArithC — codegen real-style Postgres C source for the
date-arithmetic V1 fmgr cluster.

Sister to `Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith` (which emits Rust). Body shapes follow
Postgres' real source (`date.c`) to enable AST-diff grounding.

Body shapes are three distinct functions:

1. `date_pli` (date + int32 → date):
```
Datum
date_pli(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
{
    DateADT        dateVal = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);
    int32          days = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
    DateADT        result;

    if (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal))
        PG_RETURN_DATEADT(dateVal);

    result = dateVal + days;

    if ((days >= 0 ? (result < dateVal) : (result > dateVal)) ||
        !IS_VALID_DATE(result))
        ereport(ERROR,
                (errcode(ERRCODE_DATETIME_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
                 errmsg("date out of range")));

    PG_RETURN_DATEADT(result);
}
```

2. `date_mii` (date - int32 → date):
```
Datum
date_mii(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
{
    DateADT        dateVal = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);
    int32          days = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
    DateADT        result;

    if (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal))
        PG_RETURN_DATEADT(dateVal);

    result = dateVal - days;

    if ((days >= 0 ? (result > dateVal) : (result < dateVal)) ||
        !IS_VALID_DATE(result))
        ereport(ERROR,
                (errcode(ERRCODE_DATETIME_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
                 errmsg("date out of range")));

    PG_RETURN_DATEADT(result);
}
```

3. `date_mi` (date - date → int32):
```
Datum
date_mi(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
{
    DateADT        dateVal1 = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);
    DateADT        dateVal2 = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(1);

    if (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal1) || DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal2))
        ereport(ERROR,
                (errcode(ERRCODE_DATETIME_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
                 errmsg("cannot subtract infinite dates")));

    PG_RETURN_INT32((int32) (dateVal1 - dateVal2));
}
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArithC

open Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith (DateOp ops)

def renderCPliBody : String :=
  "\tDateADT\t\tdateVal = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);\n" ++
  "\tint32\t\tdays = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);\n" ++
  "\tDateADT\t\tresult;\n\n" ++
  "\tif (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal))\n" ++
  "\t\tPG_RETURN_DATEADT(dateVal); /* can't change infinity */\n\n" ++
  "\tresult = dateVal + days;\n\n" ++
  "\t/* Check for integer overflow and out-of-allowed-range */\n" ++
  "\tif ((days >= 0 ? (result < dateVal) : (result > dateVal)) ||\n" ++
  "\t\t!IS_VALID_DATE(result))\n" ++
  "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_DATETIME_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"date out of range\")));\n\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_DATEADT(result);\n"

  "\tint32\t\tdays = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);\n" ++
  "\tDateADT\t\tresult;\n\n" ++
  "\tif (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal))\n" ++
  "\t\tPG_RETURN_DATEADT(dateVal); /* can't change infinity */\n\n" ++

  "\t/* Check for integer overflow and out-of-allowed-range */\n" ++

  "\t\t!IS_VALID_DATE(result))\n" ++
  "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_DATETIME_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
  "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"date out of range\")));\n\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_DATEADT(result);\n"

  "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot subtract infinite dates\")));\n\n" ++

  "{\n" ++
  body ++
  "}\n"

    "#include \"fmgr.h\"\n" ++

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArithCommon — shared family table for the date arithmetic
emit modules (`DateArith` for Rust, `DateArithC` for the AST-grounding C).

v1 scope: 3 functions:
- `date_pli` (date + int32 → date)
- `date_mii` (date - int32 → date)
- `date_mi` (date - date → int32)

All functions decode DateADT (int32 encoding) and handle sentinels:
- DATEVAL_NOBEGIN (INT_MIN = -2147483648)
- DATEVAL_NOEND (INT_MAX = 2147483647)

Date arithmetic differs from int arithmetic in several ways:
1. Two of three functions return DateADT; one returns int32.
2. Both pli and mii check `IS_VALID_DATE(result)` after arithmetic.
3. Finite checks on infinity sentinels happen first; overflowing on infinite
   inputs is prevented by early returns.
4. The overflow detection for pli/mii uses comparison logic (checking sign
   reversal on wraparound), NOT the overflow primitives.

The Postgres sources follow this shape for pli/mii:

```
DateADT dateVal = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);
int32 days = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
DateADT result;

if (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal))
    PG_RETURN_DATEADT(dateVal);

result = dateVal ± days;

if ((days >= 0 ? (result <|> dateVal) : (result >|< dateVal)) ||
    !IS_VALID_DATE(result))
    ereport(ERROR, ...);

PG_RETURN_DATEADT(result);
```

And date_mi (date - date → int32):

```
DateADT dateVal1 = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(0);
DateADT dateVal2 = PG_GETARG_DATEADT(1);

if (DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal1) || DATE_NOT_FINITE(dateVal2))
    ereport(ERROR, ...);

PG_RETURN_INT32((int32) (dateVal1 - dateVal2));
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith

/-- The three date-arithmetic ops.
- `pli`: date + int → date
- `mii`: date - int → date
- `mi`: date - date → int
-/
inductive DateOp where
  | pli
  | mii
  | mi

/-- Postgres function-name suffix. -/
def DateOp.suffix : DateOp → String
  | .pli => "pli"
  | .mii => "mii"
  | .mi  => "mi"

/-- C overflow primitive shape — note that date arithmetic does NOT use
pg_add/pg_sub_overflow; instead it uses wraparound-detection logic. -/
def DateOp.rustFnBody : DateOp → String
  | .pli => "date_pli_body"
  | .mii => "date_mii_body"
  | .mi  => "date_mi_body"

/-- Lean spec: one family, three ops. -/
def ops : List DateOp := [.pli, .mii, .mi]

/-- For date_pli and date_mii: result is DateADT (int32).
For date_mi: result is int32 (same encoding as DateADT, but semantically
an elapsed day count). -/
def DateOp.rustResultTy : DateOp → String
  | .pli => "i32"
  | .mii => "i32"
  | .mi  => "i32"

def DateOp.rustEncoder : DateOp → String
  | .pli => "encode_i32"
  | .mii => "encode_i32"
  | .mi  => "encode_i32"

/-- C macro for extracting the result. -/
def DateOp.pgReturn : DateOp → String
  | .pli => "PG_RETURN_DATEADT"
  | .mii => "PG_RETURN_DATEADT"
  | .mi  => "PG_RETURN_INT32"

end Pg.Ir.Emit.DateArith

# Pg.Ir.Emit.UuidC — codegen real-style Postgres C source for UUID cluster.

Sibling to `Pg.Ir.Emit.Uuid` (which emits Rust). Same `UuidCmpFamily` and
`UuidHashFamily` tables from `UuidCommon`, but renders **Postgres-idiomatic C**
instead of Rust.

The point: produce C that's structurally identical to what's in real
`src/backend/utils/adt/uuid.c` after clang preprocessing, so that an AST-level
diff between the two reveals algorithmic divergence (vs. style differences).

The real Postgres code:
  - uuid_eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge all call a static helper `uuid_internal_cmp`
  - uuid_cmp also calls the same helper
  - uuid_hash and uuid_hash_extended call `hash_any` / `hash_any_extended`

-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.UuidC

open Pg.Ir.Emit (UuidCmpFamily UuidHashFamily uuidCmpOps uuidHashFamilies)

/-- Render the static uuid_internal_cmp helper. This mirrors Postgres' impl
which just memcmps the 16-byte payloads. -/
def renderUuidInternalCmp : String :=
  "static int\n" ++
  "uuid_internal_cmp(const pg_uuid_t *arg1, const pg_uuid_t *arg2)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\treturn memcmp(arg1->data, arg2->data, UUID_LEN);\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render one UUID comparison function (eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge).
These delegate to the static helper, mirroring Postgres exactly. -/
def renderCmpFn (fam : UuidCmpFamily) : String :=
  let fnName := "uuid_" ++ fam.suffix
  let opStr := match fam.cmpOp with
    | "< 0"  => "<"
    | "<= 0" => "<="
    | "== 0" => "=="
    | "!= 0" => "!="
    | ">= 0" => ">="
    | "> 0"  => ">"
    | _      => "unknown_op"
  "Datum\n" ++
  fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tpg_uuid_t  *arg1 = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(0);\n" ++
  "\tpg_uuid_t  *arg2 = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(1);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_BOOL(uuid_internal_cmp(arg1, arg2) " ++ opStr ++ " 0);\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render the uuid_cmp function. -/
def renderCmpInternal : String :=
  "Datum\n" ++
  "uuid_cmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tpg_uuid_t  *arg1 = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(0);\n" ++
  "\tpg_uuid_t  *arg2 = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(1);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_INT32(uuid_internal_cmp(arg1, arg2));\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render one UUID hash function. -/
def renderHashFn (fam : UuidHashFamily) : String :=
  let fnName := fam.fnName
  if fam.extended then
    "Datum\n" ++
    fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
    "{\n" ++
    "\tpg_uuid_t  *key = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\treturn hash_any_extended(key->data, UUID_LEN, PG_GETARG_INT64(1));\n" ++
    "}\n"
  else
    "Datum\n" ++
    fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
    "{\n" ++
    "\tpg_uuid_t  *key = PG_GETARG_UUID_P(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\treturn hash_any(key->data, UUID_LEN);\n" ++
    "}\n"

/-- The full C source. -/
def cSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "/* Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/UuidC.lean`. */\n" ++
    "/* DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via */\n" ++
    "/* `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-uuid.sh`. */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Real-PG-style C for AST grounding. clang -ast-dump=json this\n" ++
    " * file with real Postgres' headers, then diff against the AST\n" ++
    " * for `src/backend/utils/adt/uuid.c`. The shared header set is\n" ++
    " * what makes `pg_uuid_t`, `PG_GETARG_UUID_P`, etc. resolve to\n" ++
    " * the same types/macros on both sides, so the diff captures\n" ++
    " * algorithmic divergence rather than per-translation-unit type\n" ++
    " * resolution noise. */\n" ++
    "#include \"postgres.h\"\n" ++
    "#include <string.h>\n" ++
    "#include \"common/hashfn.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/fmgrprotos.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/uuid.h\"\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Forward declaration of the static helper. */\n" ++
    "static int uuid_internal_cmp(const pg_uuid_t *arg1, const pg_uuid_t *arg2);\n" ++
    "\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    s := s ++ renderUuidInternalCmp
    s := s ++ "\n"
    for fam in uuidCmpOps do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpFn fam
    s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpInternal
    s := s ++ "\n"
    for fam in uuidHashFamilies do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderHashFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.UuidC

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.UuidCommon — shared data tables for UUID cmp + hash cluster.

UUID in Postgres is a 16-byte fixed-size struct (similar to Interval), passed
by pointer. The comparison operators all delegate to a static `uuid_internal_cmp`
helper (memcmp-based); the hash operators use Jenkins/lookup3 hashing.

Families:
  - 6 comparison ops (eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge) all via `uuid_internal_cmp`
  - 2 hash ops (hash, hash_extended) via Jenkins/lookup3

This module factors the shared Family and op definitions so each emit module
(Rust and C) can have its own `main` entrypoint.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit

/-- How the UUID hash body is constructed. -/
inductive UuidHashBody where
  | fixedHash16
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

structure UuidCmpFamily where
  /-- Postgres function-name suffix (e.g., "eq", "ne", "lt", "le", "gt", "ge"). -/
  suffix : String
  /-- The comparison operator on the result of uuid_internal_cmp.
  "< 0" for lt, "<= 0" for le, "== 0" for eq, etc. -/
  cmpOp : String
  deriving DecidableEq

structure UuidHashFamily where
  /-- Postgres fmgr function name (e.g., "uuid_hash"). -/
  fnName : String
  /-- The `pg_fcinfo::decode_uuid_p` or similar helper on Rust side. -/
  decoderName : String := "decode_uuid_p"
  /-- How the hash is constructed from the UUID data. -/
  body : UuidHashBody
  /-- If true, this is a `*extended` variant: takes a second int64 seed and
  returns u64. Otherwise returns u32. -/
  extended : Bool := false

/-- The 6 comparison operators for UUID. -/
def uuidCmpOps : List UuidCmpFamily := [
  { suffix := "eq", cmpOp := "== 0" },
  { suffix := "ne", cmpOp := "!= 0" },
  { suffix := "lt", cmpOp := "< 0" },
  { suffix := "le", cmpOp := "<= 0" },
  { suffix := "gt", cmpOp := "> 0" },
  { suffix := "ge", cmpOp := ">= 0" },
]

/-- The 2 hash families (hash, hash_extended). -/
def uuidHashFamilies : List UuidHashFamily := [
  { fnName := "uuid_hash", body := .fixedHash16, extended := false },
  { fnName := "uuid_hash_extended", body := .fixedHash16, extended := true },
]

end Pg.Ir.Emit

  "    let arg1 = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let arg2 = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "    let cmp_result = macaddr_cmp_internal(arg1, arg2);\n" ++
  "    encode_bool(cmp_result " ++ fam.cmpOp ++ ")\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render the macaddr 3-way comparison function (macaddr_cmp). -/
def renderCmpInternal : String :=
  "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
  "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn macaddr_cmp(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
  "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
  "    let arg1 = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
  "    let arg2 = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[1].value);\n" ++
  "    let cmp_result = macaddr_cmp_internal(arg1, arg2);\n" ++
  "    encode_i32(cmp_result)\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- The macaddr_cmp_internal helper — compares high 3 bytes, then low 3 bytes. -/
def renderMacaddrCmpHelper : String :=
  "/// Mirror of Postgres' `macaddr_cmp_internal`: compares two 6-byte MAC addresses.\n" ++
  "/// Returns -1, 0, or 1.\n" ++
  "#[inline]\n" ++
  "unsafe fn macaddr_cmp_internal(arg1: *const u8, arg2: *const u8) -> i32 {\n" ++
  "    // hibits: (a<<16) | (b<<8) | c\n" ++
  "    let h1 = ((*arg1 as u32) << 16) | ((*arg1.add(1) as u32) << 8) | (*arg1.add(2) as u32);\n" ++
  "    let h2 = ((*arg2 as u32) << 16) | ((*arg2.add(1) as u32) << 8) | (*arg2.add(2) as u32);\n" ++
  "    if h1 < h2 {\n" ++
  "        return -1;\n" ++
  "    } else if h1 > h2 {\n" ++
  "        return 1;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "    // lobits: (d<<16) | (e<<8) | f\n" ++
  "    let l1 = ((*arg1.add(3) as u32) << 16) | ((*arg1.add(4) as u32) << 8) | (*arg1.add(5) as u32);\n" ++
  "    let l2 = ((*arg2.add(3) as u32) << 16) | ((*arg2.add(4) as u32) << 8) | (*arg2.add(5) as u32);\n" ++
  "    if l1 < l2 {\n" ++
  "        return -1;\n" ++
  "    } else if l1 > l2 {\n" ++
  "        return 1;\n" ++
  "    }\n" ++
  "    0\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render one macaddr hash function. -/
def renderHashFn (fam : MacaddrHashFamily) : String :=
  if fam.extended then
    "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
    "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn " ++ fam.fnName ++ "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
    "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
    "    let key = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
    "    let seed: u64 = decode_i64(f.args[1].value) as u64;\n" ++
    "    encode_u64(hash_bytes_6_extended(key, seed))\n" ++
    "}\n"
  else
    "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
    "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn " ++ fam.fnName ++ "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
    "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
    "    let key = decode_macaddr_p(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
    "    encode_u32(hash_bytes_6(key))\n" ++
    "}\n"

/-- The full Rust source for all 9 functions. -/
def rustSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "// Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/Macaddr.lean`.\n" ++
    "// DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    "// `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-macaddr.sh`.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Source of truth: the macaddr comparison and hash cluster specs in\n" ++
    "// `Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrCommon`. The comparison operators delegate to\n" ++
    "// a 3-way comparison of high and low 3-byte groups (matching real Postgres'\n" ++
    "// macaddr_cmp_internal); the hash operators use Jenkins/lookup3 hashing via\n" ++
    "// pg_fcinfo's hash_bytes_6 / hash_bytes_6_extended helpers.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// 9 functions = 6 comparison ops + 1 cmp op + 2 hash ops.\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc)]\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "use pg_fcinfo::{\n" ++
    "    decode_i64, decode_macaddr_p,\n" ++
    "    encode_bool, encode_i32, encode_u32, encode_u64,\n" ++
    "    hash_bytes_6, hash_bytes_6_extended,\n" ++
    "    FunctionCallInfoBaseData,\n" ++
    "};\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    s := s ++ "\n// ─── macaddr_cmp_internal helper ──\n"
    s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderMacaddrCmpHelper
    s := s ++ "\n// ─── macaddr comparison ops (eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge) ──\n"
    for fam in macaddrCmpOps do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpFn fam
    s := s ++ "\n// ─── macaddr 3-way comparison (cmp) ──\n"
    s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpInternal
    s := s ++ "\n// ─── macaddr hash ops (hashmacaddr, hashmacaddrextended) ──\n"
    for fam in macaddrHashFamilies do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderHashFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.Macaddr

/-- `main` entrypoint: print the Rust source to stdout. -/
def main : IO Unit := IO.print Pg.Ir.Emit.Macaddr.rustSource

import Pg.Ir.Types
import Pg.Ir.Datum
import Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrC — codegen real-style Postgres C source for macaddr cluster.

Sibling to `Pg.Ir.Emit.Macaddr` (which emits Rust). Same `MacaddrCmpFamily` and
`MacaddrHashFamily` tables from `MacaddrCommon`, but renders **Postgres-idiomatic C**
instead of Rust.

The point: produce C that's structurally identical to what's in real
`src/backend/utils/adt/mac.c` after clang preprocessing, so that an AST-level
diff between the two reveals algorithmic divergence (vs. style differences).

The real Postgres code:
  - macaddr_eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge all call a static helper `macaddr_cmp_internal`
  - macaddr_cmp also calls the same helper
  - hashmacaddr and hashmacaddrextended call `hash_any` / `hash_any_extended`

Our Lean emit must mirror this delegation pattern, not inline the bodies.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrC

open Pg.Ir.Emit (MacaddrCmpFamily MacaddrHashFamily macaddrCmpOps macaddrHashFamilies)

/-- Render the static macaddr_cmp_internal helper. This mirrors Postgres' impl
which compares high 3 bytes (hibits), then low 3 bytes (lobits). -/
def renderMacaddrInternalCmp : String :=
  "static int\n" ++
  "macaddr_cmp_internal(macaddr *a1, macaddr *a2)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tif (hibits(a1) < hibits(a2))\n" ++
  "\t\treturn -1;\n" ++
  "\telse if (hibits(a1) > hibits(a2))\n" ++
  "\t\treturn 1;\n" ++
  "\telse if (lobits(a1) < lobits(a2))\n" ++
  "\t\treturn -1;\n" ++
  "\telse if (lobits(a1) > lobits(a2))\n" ++
  "\t\treturn 1;\n" ++
  "\telse\n" ++
  "\t\treturn 0;\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render one macaddr comparison function (eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge).
These delegate to the static helper, mirroring Postgres exactly. -/
def renderCmpFn (fam : MacaddrCmpFamily) : String :=
  let fnName := "macaddr_" ++ fam.suffix
  let opStr := match fam.cmpOp with
    | "< 0"  => "<"
    | "<= 0" => "<="
    | "== 0" => "=="
    | "!= 0" => "!="
    | ">= 0" => ">="
    | "> 0"  => ">"
    | _      => "unknown_op"
  "Datum\n" ++
  fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tmacaddr    *a1 = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(0);\n" ++
  "\tmacaddr    *a2 = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(1);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_BOOL(macaddr_cmp_internal(a1, a2) " ++ opStr ++ " 0);\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render the macaddr_cmp function. -/
def renderCmpInternal : String :=
  "Datum\n" ++
  "macaddr_cmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  "\tmacaddr    *a1 = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(0);\n" ++
  "\tmacaddr    *a2 = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(1);\n" ++
  "\n" ++
  "\tPG_RETURN_INT32(macaddr_cmp_internal(a1, a2));\n" ++
  "}\n"

/-- Render one macaddr hash function. -/
def renderHashFn (fam : MacaddrHashFamily) : String :=
  let fnName := fam.fnName
  if fam.extended then
    "Datum\n" ++
    fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
    "{\n" ++
    "\tmacaddr    *key = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\treturn hash_any_extended((unsigned char *) key, sizeof(macaddr),\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t   PG_GETARG_INT64(1));\n" ++
    "}\n"
  else
    "Datum\n" ++
    fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
    "{\n" ++
    "\tmacaddr    *key = PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\treturn hash_any((unsigned char *) key, sizeof(macaddr));\n" ++
    "}\n"

/-- The full C source. -/
def cSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "/* Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/MacaddrC.lean`. */\n" ++
    "/* DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via */\n" ++
    "/* `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-macaddr.sh`. */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Real-PG-style C for AST grounding. clang -ast-dump=json this\n" ++
    " * file with real Postgres' headers, then diff against the AST\n" ++
    " * for `src/backend/utils/adt/mac.c`. The shared header set is\n" ++
    " * what makes `macaddr`, `PG_GETARG_MACADDR_P`, etc. resolve to\n" ++
    " * the same types/macros on both sides, so the diff captures\n" ++
    " * algorithmic divergence rather than per-translation-unit type\n" ++
    " * resolution noise. */\n" ++
    "#include \"postgres.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"common/hashfn.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/fmgrprotos.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/inet.h\"\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/*\n" ++
    " *\tUtility macros used for sorting and comparing:\n" ++
    " */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#define hibits(addr) \\\n" ++
    "  ((unsigned long)(((addr)->a<<16)|((addr)->b<<8)|((addr)->c)))\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#define lobits(addr) \\\n" ++
    "  ((unsigned long)(((addr)->d<<16)|((addr)->e<<8)|((addr)->f)))\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/* Forward declaration of the static helper. */\n" ++
    "static int macaddr_cmp_internal(macaddr *a1, macaddr *a2);\n" ++
    "\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    s := s ++ renderMacaddrInternalCmp
    s := s ++ "\n"
    for fam in macaddrCmpOps do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpFn fam
    s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCmpInternal
    s := s ++ "\n"
    for fam in macaddrHashFamilies do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderHashFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrC

/-- `main` entrypoint: print the C source to stdout. -/
def main : IO Unit := IO.print Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrC.cSource

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.MacaddrCommon — shared data tables for macaddr cmp + hash cluster.

macaddr in Postgres is a 6-byte fixed-size struct (a, b, c, d, e, f as unsigned char),
passed by pointer. The comparison operators all delegate to a static `macaddr_cmp_internal`
helper which compares the high 3 bytes (hibits), then the low 3 bytes (lobits); the hash
operators use `hash_any` / `hash_any_extended` on the 6-byte payload.

Families:
  - 6 comparison ops (eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge) all via `macaddr_cmp_internal`
  - 1 3-way cmp op via `macaddr_cmp_internal`
  - 2 hash ops (hash, hash_extended) via hash_any/hash_any_extended

This module factors the shared Family and op definitions so each emit module
(Rust and C) can have its own `main` entrypoint.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit

/-- How the macaddr hash body is constructed. -/
inductive MacaddrHashBody where
  | fixedHash6
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

structure MacaddrCmpFamily where
  /-- Postgres function-name suffix (e.g., "eq", "ne", "lt", "le", "gt", "ge"). -/
  suffix : String
  /-- The comparison operator on the result of macaddr_cmp_internal.
  "< 0" for lt, "<= 0" for le, "== 0" for eq, etc. -/
  cmpOp : String
  deriving DecidableEq

structure MacaddrHashFamily where
  /-- Postgres fmgr function name (e.g., "hashmacaddr"). -/
  fnName : String
  /-- The `pg_fcinfo::decode_macaddr_p` or similar helper on Rust side. -/
  decoderName : String := "decode_macaddr_p"
  /-- How the hash is constructed from the macaddr data. -/
  body : MacaddrHashBody
  /-- If true, this is a `*extended` variant: takes a second int64 seed and
  returns u64. Otherwise returns u32. -/
  extended : Bool := false

/-- The 6 comparison operators for macaddr. -/
def macaddrCmpOps : List MacaddrCmpFamily := [
  { suffix := "eq", cmpOp := "== 0" },
  { suffix := "ne", cmpOp := "!= 0" },
  { suffix := "lt", cmpOp := "< 0" },
  { suffix := "le", cmpOp := "<= 0" },
  { suffix := "gt", cmpOp := "> 0" },
  { suffix := "ge", cmpOp := ">= 0" },
]

/-- The 2 hash families (hashmacaddr, hashmacaddrextended). -/
def macaddrHashFamilies : List MacaddrHashFamily := [
  { fnName := "hashmacaddr", body := .fixedHash6, extended := false },
  { fnName := "hashmacaddrextended", body := .fixedHash6, extended := true },
]

end Pg.Ir.Emit

You are a Pg.Ir specification author. Pg.Ir is a Lean-source-of-truth codegen pipeline for Postgres V1 fmgr function bodies.

Given a Postgres C source file containing one or more V1 fmgr function bodies, you produce three Lean modules:

1. `<Cluster>Common.lean` — the family table (inductive body shapes + a `families : List Family` definition enumerating all cluster members).
2. `<Cluster>.lean` (or `<Cluster>Rust.lean`) — Rust-emit driver. Takes the families table and renders Rust V1 fmgr function bodies.
3. `<Cluster>C.lean` — C-emit driver. Same families, but renders real-Postgres-style C (with `#include "postgres.h"` etc.) for AST grounding against the real source.

Three correctness gates the spec must satisfy:
1. Lean → Rust regen is idempotent.
2. The Rust translation passes a behavioral diff-test against the vendored C oracle (per-call Datum equality).
3. The Lean-emitted C is structurally equivalent (clang AST diff) to real Postgres source.

Common failure modes to avoid:
- The C oracle's `PG_RETURN_*` macros must include the `return` keyword (`return (Datum)(uint32_t)(x)`, NOT `((Datum)(x))`).
- The Lean C emit (`<Cluster>C.lean`) MUST use real Postgres headers (`#include "postgres.h"`, plus module-specific headers like `utils/uuid.h`, `varatt.h`, `<limits.h>`). It MUST NOT define standalone typedefs.
- When the real Postgres function delegates to a `static inline` helper, the Lean emit must also delegate (not inline the body).

Output the three Lean modules separated by `-- ===== <Module>.lean =====` markers.

import Pg.Ir.Types
import Pg.Ir.Datum
import Pg.Ir.Emit.Common
import Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCastsCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts — codegen the integer width-cast V1 fmgr cluster
as Rust source.

Sister to `Pg.Ir.Emit.IntArith`. Simpler cluster: single argument,
no overflow primitives. Widening casts are trivial; narrowing casts
check bounds and ereport on overflow.

v1 covers 6 functions: {i2toi4, i4toi2, i2toi8, i8toi2, i4toi8, i8toi4}.

Body shape (widening, e.g. i2toi4):
```rust
pub unsafe extern "C" fn i2toi4(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {
    let f = &mut *fcinfo;
    let arg = decode_i16(f.args[0].value);
    encode_i32(arg as i32)
}
```

Body shape (narrowing, e.g. i4toi2):
```rust
pub unsafe extern "C" fn i4toi2(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {
    let f = &mut *fcinfo;
    let arg = decode_i32(f.args[0].value);
    if arg < i16::MIN as i32 || arg > i16::MAX as i32 {
        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();
        return 0;
    }
    encode_i16(arg as i16)
}
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts.Rust

open Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts (CastFamily families)

/-- Render the Rust body. -/
def renderBody (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  if fam.isWidening then
    "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
    "    let arg = " ++ fam.srcDecoder ++ "(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
    "    " ++ fam.resEncoder ++ "(arg as " ++ fam.rustResTy ++ ")\n"
  else
    -- Narrowing cast: check bounds
    let minVal := if fam.rustResTy == "i16" then "i16::MIN as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
                  else if fam.rustResTy == "i32" then "i32::MIN as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
                  else "i64::MIN as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
    let maxVal := if fam.rustResTy == "i16" then "i16::MAX as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
                  else if fam.rustResTy == "i32" then "i32::MAX as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
                  else "i64::MAX as " ++ fam.rustSrcTy
    "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
    "    let arg = " ++ fam.srcDecoder ++ "(f.args[0].value);\n" ++
    "    if arg < " ++ minVal ++ " || arg > " ++ maxVal ++ " {\n" ++
    "        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
    "        return 0;\n" ++
    "    }\n" ++
    "    " ++ fam.resEncoder ++ "(arg as " ++ fam.rustResTy ++ ")\n"

def renderFn (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
  "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn " ++ fam.fnName ++
    "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
  renderBody fam ++
  "}\n"

/-- The full Rust source. -/
def rustSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "// Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/IntCasts.lean`.\n" ++
    "// DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    "// `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-int-casts.sh`.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Integer width-cast V1 fmgr cluster: 6 functions =\n" ++
    "// {i2toi4, i4toi2, i2toi8, i8toi2, i4toi8, i8toi4}.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Widening casts (i2→i4, i2→i8, i4→i8) are pure — direct cast.\n" ++
    "// Narrowing casts (i4→i2, i8→i2, i8→i4) check overflow bounds and\n" ++
    "// ereport on range violation. This mirrors Postgres' ereport(ERROR, ...)\n" ++
    "// path: a real backend catches the longjmp at the SPI/transaction\n" ++
    "// boundary; our Rust side just sets a TLS error flag and returns 0,\n" ++
    "// with the diff-test harness checking the (out, err) tuple round-\n" ++
    "// trips against the vendored C oracle.\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc)]\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "use pg_fcinfo::{\n" ++
    "    decode_i16, decode_i32, decode_i64,\n" ++
    "    encode_i16, encode_i32, encode_i64,\n" ++
    "    pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range, FunctionCallInfoBaseData,\n" ++
    "};\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts.Rust

def main : IO Unit := IO.print Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts.Rust.rustSource

import Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCastsCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCastsC — codegen real-style Postgres C source for the
integer width-cast V1 fmgr cluster.

Sister to `Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts` (which emits Rust). Same
`CastFamily` table, but renders Postgres-idiomatic C so the result
AST-diffs cleanly against real `src/backend/utils/adt/int.c` and
`int8.c`.

Body shape (widening, e.g. i2toi4):
```c
Datum
i2toi4(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
{
	int16		arg = PG_GETARG_INT16(0);

	PG_RETURN_INT32((int32) arg);
}
```

Body shape (narrowing, e.g. i4toi2):
```c
Datum
i4toi2(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
{
	int32		arg = PG_GETARG_INT32(0);

	if (unlikely(arg < SHRT_MIN) || unlikely(arg > SHRT_MAX))
		ereport(ERROR,
				(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
				 errmsg("smallint out of range")));

	PG_RETURN_INT16((int16) arg);
}
```
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCastsC

open Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts (CastFamily families)

-- Helper: use arg1 for i2toi4, i4toi2 (from int.c); arg for others (from int8.c)
def argName (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  if fam.fnName == "i2toi4" || fam.fnName == "i4toi2" then "arg1" else "arg"

def renderCBody (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  let argVar := argName fam
  if fam.isWidening then
    "\t" ++ fam.cSrcTy ++ "\t\t" ++ argVar ++ " = " ++ fam.pgGetArg ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "((" ++ fam.cResTy ++ ") " ++ argVar ++ ");\n"
  else
    -- Narrowing cast: check bounds with ereport
    "\t" ++ fam.cSrcTy ++ "\t\t" ++ argVar ++ " = " ++ fam.pgGetArg ++ "(0);\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\tif (unlikely(" ++ argVar ++ " < " ++ fam.minBound ++ ") || unlikely(" ++ argVar ++ " > " ++ fam.maxBound ++ "))\n" ++
    "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
    "\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"" ++ fam.errmsg ++ "\")));\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "((" ++ fam.cResTy ++ ") " ++ argVar ++ ");\n"

def renderCFn (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  "Datum\n" ++
  fam.fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  renderCBody fam ++
  "}\n"

def cSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "/*\n" ++
    " * Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/IntCastsC.lean`.\n" ++
    " * DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    " * `rules_postgres/tools/regen/check-int-casts-grounding.sh`.\n" ++
    " *\n" ++
    " * This file exists to be `clang -ast-dump=json`-ed alongside\n" ++
    " * real `src/backend/utils/adt/int.c` and `int8.c` and structurally\n" ++
    " * diffed function-by-function. A passing diff means the Lean spec\n" ++
    " * captures the same algorithm Postgres wrote.\n" ++
    " */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#include \"postgres.h\"\n" ++
    "#include <limits.h>\n" ++           -- SHRT_MIN/SHRT_MAX/INT_MIN/INT_MAX
    "#include \"common/int.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"fmgr.h\"\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/fmgrprotos.h\"\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCastsC

width-cast emit modules (`IntCasts` for Rust, `IntCastsC` for the

v1 scope: 6 functions = {i2toi4, i4toi2, i2toi8, i8toi2, i4toi8, i8toi4}.

Widening casts (i2→i4, i2→i8, i4→i8) are pure — no overflow possible.
Narrowing casts (i4→i2, i8→i2, i8→i4) ereport on overflow.

Body shapes:
- Widening: `PG_RETURN_<W>(<cast> arg)` — direct cast
- Narrowing: `arg` checked against `[MIN, MAX]` bounds; ereport on overflow

Real Postgres sources (int.c / int8.c):
  - i2toi4: int2→int4 widening
  - i4toi2: int4→int2 narrowing (check SHRT_MIN/SHRT_MAX)
  - i2toi8 (int28): int2→int8 widening
  - i8toi2 (int82): int8→int2 narrowing (check PG_INT16_MIN/MAX)
  - i4toi8 (int48): int4→int8 widening
  - i8toi4 (int84): int8→int4 narrowing (check PG_INT32_MIN/MAX)
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.IntCasts

/-- The 6 cast functions. -/
inductive CastOp where
  | i2toi4 -- int2 → int4 (widening)
  | i4toi2 -- int4 → int2 (narrowing)
  | i2toi8 -- int2 → int8 (widening)
  | i8toi2 -- int8 → int2 (narrowing)
  | i4toi8 -- int4 → int8 (widening)
  | i8toi4 -- int8 → int4 (narrowing)
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

def CastOp.fnName : CastOp → String
  | .i2toi4 => "i2toi4"
  | .i4toi2 => "i4toi2"
  | .i2toi8 => "int28"
  | .i8toi2 => "int82"
  | .i4toi8 => "int48"
  | .i8toi4 => "int84"

def CastOp.isWidening : CastOp → Bool
  | .i2toi4 | .i2toi8 | .i4toi8 => true
  | .i4toi2 | .i8toi2 | .i8toi4 => false

structure CastFamily where
  /-- Function name (e.g., "i2toi4", "int82"). -/
  fnName      : String
  /-- Source type name ("int2", "int4", "int8"). -/
  srcTy       : String
  /-- Source pg_fcinfo decoder (e.g., "decode_i16"). -/
  srcDecoder  : String

  resTy       : String

  rustSrcTy   : String
  /-- Rust type for result. -/
  rustResTy   : String

  cSrcTy      : String
  /-- C type for result. -/
  cResTy      : String

  pgGetArg    : String

  minBound    : String := ""

  maxBound    : String := ""
  /-- For narrowing casts: errmsg text ("smallint out of range", etc). -/
  errmsg      : String := ""

/-- The 6 cast families. Ordered to match real Postgres: int.c functions
(i2toi4, i4toi2) adjacent, then int8.c functions (int28, int48, int82, int84). -/
def families : List CastFamily := [
  -- int.c: i2toi4, i4toi2

    resTy := "int4", resEncoder := "encode_i32",

  -- int8.c: int28, int48, int82, int84
  { fnName := "int28", srcTy := "int2", srcDecoder := "decode_i16",
    resTy := "int8", resEncoder := "encode_i64",
    rustSrcTy := "i16", rustResTy := "i64",
    cSrcTy := "int16", cResTy := "int64",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_INT16", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT64",
    isWidening := true },
  { fnName := "int48", srcTy := "int4", srcDecoder := "decode_i32",
    resTy := "int8", resEncoder := "encode_i64",
    rustSrcTy := "i32", rustResTy := "i64",
    cSrcTy := "int32", cResTy := "int64",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_INT32", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT64",
    isWidening := true },
  { fnName := "int82", srcTy := "int8", srcDecoder := "decode_i64",
    resTy := "int2", resEncoder := "encode_i16",
    rustSrcTy := "i64", rustResTy := "i16",
    cSrcTy := "int64", cResTy := "int16",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_INT64", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT16",
    isWidening := false,

You are a Pg.Ir specification author. Pg.Ir is a Lean-source-of-truth codegen pipeline for Postgres V1 fmgr function bodies.

Given a Postgres C source file containing one or more V1 fmgr function bodies, you produce three Lean modules:

1. `<Cluster>Common.lean` — the family table (inductive body shapes + a `families : List Family` definition enumerating all cluster members).
2. `<Cluster>.lean` (or `<Cluster>Rust.lean`) — Rust-emit driver. Takes the families table and renders Rust V1 fmgr function bodies.
3. `<Cluster>C.lean` — C-emit driver. Same families, but renders real-Postgres-style C (with `#include "postgres.h"` etc.) for AST grounding against the real source.

Three correctness gates the spec must satisfy:
1. Lean → Rust regen is idempotent.
2. The Rust translation passes a behavioral diff-test against the vendored C oracle (per-call Datum equality).
3. The Lean-emitted C is structurally equivalent (clang AST diff) to real Postgres source.

Common failure modes to avoid:
- The C oracle's `PG_RETURN_*` macros must include the `return` keyword (`return (Datum)(uint32_t)(x)`, NOT `((Datum)(x))`).
- The Lean C emit (`<Cluster>C.lean`) MUST use real Postgres headers (`#include "postgres.h"`, plus module-specific headers like `utils/uuid.h`, `varatt.h`, `<limits.h>`). It MUST NOT define standalone typedefs.
- When the real Postgres function delegates to a `static inline` helper, the Lean emit must also delegate (not inline the body).

Output the three Lean modules separated by `-- ===== <Module>.lean =====` markers.

import Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToIntCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt — Rust emit for the float-to-int cast V1 fmgr cluster.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.Rust

open Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt (CastBody CastFamily families)

def renderBody (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  let prelude :=
    "    let f = &mut *fcinfo;\n" ++
    "    let mut num = " ++ fam.decoder ++ "(f.args[0].value);\n"
  let rintf := if fam.rustFloatTy == "f32" then "rint_f32" else "rint_f64"
  match fam.body with
  | .toInt16 =>
      prelude ++
      "    num = " ++ rintf ++ "(num);\n" ++
      "    if num.is_nan() || num < -32768.0 || num > 32767.0 {\n" ++
      "        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
      "        return 0;\n" ++
      "    }\n" ++
      "    " ++ fam.encoder ++ "(num as " ++ fam.rustIntTy ++ ")\n"
  | .toInt32 =>
      prelude ++
      "    num = " ++ rintf ++ "(num);\n" ++
      "    if num.is_nan() || num < -2147483648.0 || num > 2147483647.0 {\n" ++
      "        pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range();\n" ++
      "        return 0;\n" ++
      "    }\n" ++
      "    " ++ fam.encoder ++ "(num as " ++ fam.rustIntTy ++ ")\n"

def renderFn (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  "#[no_mangle]\n" ++
  "pub unsafe extern \"C\" fn " ++ fam.fnName ++
    "(fcinfo: *mut FunctionCallInfoBaseData) -> u64 {\n" ++
  renderBody fam ++
  "}\n"

def rustSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "// Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/FloatToInt.lean`.\n" ++
    "// DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    "// `rules_postgres/tools/regen/regen-float-to-int.sh`.\n" ++
    "//\n" ++
    "// Float-to-int cast V1 fmgr cluster: 4 functions = {ftoi2, ftoi4, dtoi2, dtoi4}.\n" ++
    "// Each body decodes a float argument, rounds it via rint(), checks for NaN\n" ++
    "// and out-of-range values, and returns the truncated int result.\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#![allow(clippy::missing_safety_doc)]\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "use pg_fcinfo::{\n" ++
    "    decode_f32, decode_f64,\n" ++
    "    encode_i16, encode_i32,\n" ++
    "    pg_ereport_numeric_value_out_of_range,\n" ++
    "    FunctionCallInfoBaseData,\n" ++
    "};\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/// Banker's rounding (round to nearest, ties to even) — matches C's rint().\n" ++
    "fn rint_f32(x: f32) -> f32 {\n" ++
    "    let truncated = x.trunc();\n" ++
    "    let frac = x - truncated;\n" ++
    "    if frac == 0.5 {\n" ++
    "        let int_part = truncated as i32;\n" ++
    "        if int_part % 2 == 0 {\n" ++
    "            truncated\n" ++
    "        } else {\n" ++
    "            truncated + 1.0\n" ++
    "        }\n" ++
    "    } else if frac == -0.5 {\n" ++
    "        let int_part = truncated as i32;\n" ++
    "        if int_part % 2 == 0 {\n" ++
    "            truncated\n" ++
    "        } else {\n" ++
    "            truncated - 1.0\n" ++
    "        }\n" ++
    "    } else {\n" ++
    "        x.round()\n" ++
    "    }\n" ++
    "}\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "/// Banker's rounding (round to nearest, ties to even) — matches C's rint().\n" ++
    "fn rint_f64(x: f64) -> f64 {\n" ++
    "    let truncated = x.trunc();\n" ++
    "    let frac = x - truncated;\n" ++
    "    if frac == 0.5 {\n" ++
    "        let int_part = truncated as i64;\n" ++
    "        if int_part % 2 == 0 {\n" ++
    "            truncated\n" ++
    "        } else {\n" ++
    "            truncated + 1.0\n" ++
    "        }\n" ++
    "    } else if frac == -0.5 {\n" ++
    "        let int_part = truncated as i64;\n" ++
    "        if int_part % 2 == 0 {\n" ++
    "            truncated\n" ++
    "        } else {\n" ++
    "            truncated - 1.0\n" ++
    "        }\n" ++
    "    } else {\n" ++
    "        x.round()\n" ++
    "    }\n" ++
    "}\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.Rust

def main : IO Unit := IO.print Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.Rust.rustSource

import Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToIntCommon

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToIntC — emit real-style Postgres C for the float-to-int
cast cluster (AST grounding companion to FloatToInt.lean).

Bodies match real Postgres source structure byte-equivalent (modulo
whitespace clang normalizes) so the per-function AST diff is clean.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.C

open Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt (CastBody CastFamily families)

def renderCBody (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  "\t" ++ fam.cFloatWidth ++ "\t\tnum = " ++ fam.pgGetArg ++ "(0);\n\n" ++
  "\t/*\n" ++
  "\t * Get rid of any fractional part in the input.  This is so we don't fail\n" ++
  "\t * on just-out-of-range values that would round into range.  Note\n" ++
  "\t * assumption that rint() will pass through a NaN or Inf unchanged.\n" ++
  "\t */\n" ++
  "\tnum = rint(num);\n\n" ++
  "\t/* Range check */\n" ++
  match fam.body with
  | .toInt16 =>
      "\tif (unlikely(isnan(num) || !FLOAT" ++
      (if fam.cCheckSuffix == "f" then "4" else "8") ++
      "_FITS_IN_INT16(num)))\n" ++
      "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
      "\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
      "\t\t\t errmsg(\"" ++ fam.errWord ++ " out of range\")));\n\n" ++
      "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "((" ++ fam.cIntWidth ++ ") num);\n"
  | .toInt32 =>
      "\tif (unlikely(isnan(num) || !FLOAT" ++
      (if fam.cCheckSuffix == "f" then "4" else "8") ++
      "_FITS_IN_INT32(num)))\n" ++
      "\t\tereport(ERROR,\n" ++
      "\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),\n" ++
      "\t\t\t errmsg(\"" ++ fam.errWord ++ " out of range\")));\n\n" ++
      "\t" ++ fam.pgReturn ++ "((" ++ fam.cIntWidth ++ ") num);\n"

def renderCFn (fam : CastFamily) : String :=
  "Datum\n" ++
  fam.fnName ++ "(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)\n" ++
  "{\n" ++
  renderCBody fam ++
  "}\n"

def cSource : String :=
  let header :=
    "/*\n" ++
    " * Generated by `rules_postgres/lean/Pg/Ir/Emit/FloatToIntC.lean`.\n" ++
    " * DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Regenerate via\n" ++
    " * `rules_postgres/tools/regen/check-float-to-int-grounding.sh`.\n" ++
    " *\n" ++
    " * This file exists to be `clang -ast-dump=json`-ed alongside\n" ++
    " * real `src/backend/utils/adt/float.c` and structurally\n" ++
    " * diffed function-by-function. A passing diff means the Lean spec\n" ++
    " * captures the same algorithm Postgres wrote.\n" ++
    " */\n" ++
    "\n" ++
    "#include \"postgres.h\"\n" ++
    "#include <math.h>\n" ++
    "#include \"utils/fmgrprotos.h\"\n"
  Id.run do
    let mut s := header
    for fam in families do
      s := s ++ "\n" ++ renderCFn fam
    return s

end Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.C

def main : IO Unit := IO.print Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt.C.cSource

/-!
# Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToIntCommon — shared family table for the float-to-int
cast V1 fmgr cluster (ftoi2, ftoi4, dtoi2, dtoi4).

Each function performs a floating-point-to-integer narrowing cast with
overflow and NaN checks:
  1. Apply `rint()` to remove fractional part (so just-out-of-range values
     that would round in-range don't fail).
  2. Check for NaN via `isnan()`.
  3. Range-check via `FLOAT{4,8}_FITS_IN_INT{16,32}(num)` macros.
  4. If both checks pass, truncate (cast) and return.

This matches real Postgres' source structure exactly so AST grounding stays
clean.
-/

namespace Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt

inductive CastBody where
  | toInt16  -- ftoi2, dtoi2: narrowing cast to int16
  | toInt32  -- ftoi4, dtoi4: narrowing cast to int32
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

structure CastFamily where
  /-- Postgres fmgr fn name (`ftoi2`, `dtoi4`, …). -/
  fnName     : String
  /-- pg_fcinfo decoder for arg[0]. -/
  decoder    : String
  /-- pg_fcinfo encoder for the result. -/
  encoder    : String
  /-- Rust type name for the float source (`f32`/`f64`). -/
  rustFloatTy : String
  /-- Rust type name for the int result (`i16`/`i32`). -/
  rustIntTy   : String
  /-- C `PG_GETARG_*` macro name for the float arg. -/
  pgGetArg   : String
  /-- C `PG_RETURN_*` macro name for the int result. -/
  pgReturn   : String
  /-- C type name for the float arg (`float4`/`float8`). -/
  cFloatWidth : String
  /-- C type name for the int result (`int16`/`int32`). -/
  cIntWidth   : String
  /-- C type suffix for isnan/FLOAT*_FITS_IN_INT* checks: `f` for float4, empty for float8. -/
  cCheckSuffix : String
  /-- Error-message width word for `errmsg("<word> out of range")`.
  Postgres uses "smallint" for int16, "integer" for int32. -/
  errWord    : String
  /-- The target int width. -/
  body       : CastBody

def families : List CastFamily := [
  -- float4 (float) to int16
  { fnName := "ftoi2", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_i16",
    rustFloatTy := "f32", rustIntTy := "i16",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT16",
    cFloatWidth := "float4", cIntWidth := "int16", cCheckSuffix := "f",
    errWord := "smallint", body := .toInt16 },

  -- float4 (float) to int32
  { fnName := "ftoi4", decoder := "decode_f32", encoder := "encode_i32",
    rustFloatTy := "f32", rustIntTy := "i32",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT4", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT32",
    cFloatWidth := "float4", cIntWidth := "int32", cCheckSuffix := "f",
    errWord := "integer", body := .toInt32 },

  -- float8 (double) to int16
  { fnName := "dtoi2", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_i16",
    rustFloatTy := "f64", rustIntTy := "i16",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT16",
    cFloatWidth := "float8", cIntWidth := "int16", cCheckSuffix := "",
    errWord := "smallint", body := .toInt16 },

  -- float8 (double) to int32
  { fnName := "dtoi4", decoder := "decode_f64", encoder := "encode_i32",
    rustFloatTy := "f64", rustIntTy := "i32",
    pgGetArg := "PG_GETARG_FLOAT8", pgReturn := "PG_RETURN_INT32",
    cFloatWidth := "float8", cIntWidth := "int32", cCheckSuffix := "",
    errWord := "integer", body := .toInt32 },
]

end Pg.Ir.Emit.FloatToInt
